question_text: Which of the following best describes what happens in a series circuit when one component fails?

options.label: A
options.text: All other components continue to work normally

options.label: B
options.text: The entire circuit stops working because the path is broken

options.label: C
options.text: Only the components next to the failed one stop working

options.label: D
options.text: The current flows backwards through the circuit

explanation: In a series circuit, all components are connected in a single loop. If one component breaks, the circuit is no longer complete, so current cannot flow anywhere. This means all components stop working. This is different from a parallel circuit, where other branches can still work.
